articles.title: Foxconn Confirms North American Factories Hit by Cyberattack
articles.summary: The Nitrogen ransomware group claims to have hacked the company’s systems, stealing 8TB of data, including confidential documents. The post Foxconn Confirms North American Factories Hit by Cyberattack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Windows BitLocker zero-day gives access to protected drives, PoC released
articles.summary: A cybersecurity researcher has published proof-of-concept (PoC) exploits for two unpatched Microsoft Windows vulnerabilities named YellowKey and GreenPlasma, which are a BitLocker bypass and a privilege-escalation flaw. [...]

articles.title: Microsoft, Palo Alto Networks Find Many Vulnerabilities by Using AI on Their Own Code
articles.summary: Microsoft’s MDASH discovered 16 of the Patch Tuesday vulnerabilities, and Palo Alto used Mythos to find dozens of flaws. The post Microsoft, Palo Alto Networks Find Many Vulnerabilities by Using AI on Their Own Code appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Webinar tomorrow: Why security alone won't stop modern attacks
articles.summary: Tomorrow's webinar examines why prevention alone is no longer enough against modern cyberattacks. The session explores how organizations combine security, backups, and recovery planning to improve cyber resilience after attacks. [...]

articles.title: Sweet Security Launches Agentic AI Red Teaming to Counter ‘Mythos Moment’
articles.summary: New “Sweet Attack” platform uses runtime intelligence and continuous agentic red teaming to identify exploitable attack chains human teams may miss. The post Sweet Security Launches Agentic AI Red Teaming to Counter ‘Mythos Moment’ appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Microsoft's MDASH AI System Finds 16 Windows Flaws Fixed in Patch Tuesday
articles.summary: Microsoft has unveiled a new multi-model artificial intelligence (AI)-driven system called MDASH to facilitate vulnerability discovery and remediation at scale, adding that it's being tested by some customers as part of a limited private preview. MDASH, short for multi-model agentic scanning harness

articles.title: Azerbaijani Energy Firm Hit by Repeated Microsoft Exchange Exploitation
articles.summary: A threat actor with affiliations to China has been linked to a "multi-wave intrusion" targeting an unnamed Azerbaijani oil and gas company between late December 2025 and late February 2026, marking an expansion of its targeting. articles.title: UK moves to shield security researchers in cybercrime law overhaul
articles.summary: The proposed reforms, outlined in briefing documents published alongside the King’s Speech opening a new parliamentary session, would update the Computer Misuse Act 1990 as part of a broader national security package focused on cybercrime and digital threats.

articles.title: Microsoft on pace to break annual vulnerability record as AI-driven patch wave takes hold
articles.summary: Five months into 2026, Microsoft has already patched more than 500 vulnerabilities — although the exact monthly count varies depending on whether analysts include Edge, Chromium and fixes shipped earlier in the month.

articles.title: 73 Seconds to Breach, 24 Hours to Patch: The Case for Autonomous Validation
articles.summary: Attackers can compromise systems in minutes while patching and response still take hours or days. Picus Security breaks down why autonomous validation is becoming critical for modern defense strategies. [...]

articles.title: Instructure confirms data breach, ShinyHunters claims attack
articles.summary: Educational tech giant Instructure has confirmed that data was stolen in a cyberattack, with the ShinyHunters extortion gang claiming responsibility. [...]

articles.title: Wireshark 4.6.5 Released, (Sun, May 3rd)
articles.summary: Wireshark release 4.6.5 fixes 43 vulnerabilities (38 CVEs) and 35 bugs.

articles.title: Telegram Mini Apps abused for crypto scams, Android malware delivery
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered a large-scale fraud operation that uses Telegram's Mini App feature to run crypto scams, impersonate well-known brands, and distribute Android malware. [...]

articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Friday added a recently disclosed security flaw impacting various Linux distributions to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation in the wild. articles.title: Critrical cPanel flaw mass-exploited in "Sorry" ransomware attacks
articles.summary: A new disclosed cPanel flaw tracked as CVE-2026-41940 is being mass-exploited to breach websites and encrypt data in "Sorry" ransomware attacks. [...]

articles.title: ConsentFix v3 attacks target Azure with automated OAuth abuse
articles.summary: A new attack type, dubbed ConsentFix v3, has been circulating on hacker forums, building on the previous technique by adding automation and scaling potential. [...]

articles.title: Trellix Confirms Source Code Breach With Unauthorized Repository Access
articles.summary: Cybersecurity company Trellix has announced that it suffered a breach that enabled unauthorized access to a "portion" of its source code. articles.summary: A newly discovered Vietnamese-linked operation has been observed using a Google AppSheet as a "phishing relay" to distribute phishing emails with an aim to compromise Facebook accounts. articles.title: 15-year-old detained over French govt agency data breach
articles.summary: French authorities have detained a 15-year-old suspected of selling data stolen in a cyberattack on France Titres (ANTS), the country's agency for issuing and managing administrative documents. [...]

articles.title: Story retracted
articles.summary: BleepingComputer initially published a story about a new data breach at Instructure. Shortly after publication, we determined that the information was incorrect and primarily based on outdated details from a prior incident. The article has been retracted, and we regret the error. [...]

articles.title: Federal agencies must patch cPanel bug by Sunday, CISA says
articles.summary: Incident responders at Rapid7 said successful exploitation of CVE-2026-41940 “grants an attacker control over the cPanel host system, its configurations and databases, and websites it manages.”

articles.title: Google Adjusts Bug Bounties: Chrome Payouts Drop as Android Rewards Rise Amid AI Surge
articles.summary: The maximum reward for a zero-click Pixel Titan M exploit with persistence has increased to $1.5 million. The post Google Adjusts Bug Bounties: Chrome Payouts Drop as Android Rewards Rise Amid AI Surge appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Cybercrime Groups Using Vishing and SSO Abuse in Rapid SaaS Extortion Attacks
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers are warning of two cybercrime groups that are carrying out "rapid, high-impact attacks" operating almost within the confines of SaaS environments, while leaving minimal traces of their actions. articles.title: Cyber incident responders who carried out ransomware attacks given 4-year sentences
articles.summary: Two cybersecurity incident responders who abused their positions to carry out covert ransomware attacks were sentenced to four years in prison.

articles.title: Criminal IP and Securonix ThreatQ Collaborate to Enhance Threat Intelligence Operations
articles.summary: Raw threat intel isn't enough without real-world context. Criminal IP has partnered with Securonix to integrate exposure-based intelligence into ThreatQ, automating analysis and speeding up investigations. [...]

articles.title: China-Linked Hackers Target Asian Governments, NATO State, Journalists, and Activists
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new China-aligned espionage campaign targeting government and defense sectors across South, East, and Southeast Asia, along with one European government belonging to NATO. articles.title: British cyber agency warns of looming ‘patch wave’ as AI speeds flaw discovery
articles.summary: Britain’s cyber agency warned that organizations should prepare for a surge of urgent software updates as artificial intelligence accelerates the discovery of security flaws, raising the risk of widespread exploitation.

articles.title: Cyber spies target Russian aviation firms to steal satellite and GPS data
articles.summary: A cyber-espionage group has been targeting Russian government agencies and companies in the aviation industry to steal sensitive geospatial data.

articles.title: Nearly every Linux system built since 2017 vulnerable to ‘Copy Fail’ flaw
articles.summary: Security researchers and European cybersecurity officials are urging administrators to address the risk posed by a newly discovered security flaw that has been hiding in the Linux operating system for nearly a decade.

articles.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
articles.summary: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-31431 Linux Kernel Incorrect Resource Transfer Between Spheres Vulnerability This type of vulnerability is a frequent attack vector for malicious cyber actors

articles.title: Two US Security Experts Sentenced to Prison for Helping Ransomware Gang
articles.summary: Ryan Goldberg of Georgia and Kevin Martin of Texas were each sentenced to four years in prison. The post Two US Security Experts Sentenced to Prison for Helping Ransomware Gang appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.summary: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Thursday announced the sentencing of two cybersecurity professionals to four years each in prison for their role in facilitating BlackCat ransomware attacks in 2023. articles.summary: A new software supply chain attack campaign has been observed using sleeper packages as a conduit to subsequently push malicious payloads that enabled credential theft, GitHub Actions tampering, and SSH persistence. The activity has been attributed to the GitHub account "BufferZoneCorp," which has p

articles.title: Hugging Face, ClawHub Abused for Malware Distribution
articles.summary: Threat actors are relying on social engineering to lure users into downloading files containing malicious instructions. The post Hugging Face, ClawHub Abused for Malware Distribution appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: FBI Warns of Surge in Hacker-Enabled Cargo Theft
articles.summary: A new alert from the FBI says criminal enterprises are hacking both brokers and carriers to steal cargo for resale. The post FBI Warns of Surge in Hacker-Enabled Cargo Theft appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: US ransomware negotiators get 4 years in prison over BlackCat attacks
articles.summary: Two former employees of cybersecurity incident response companies Sygnia and DigitalMint were sentenced to four years in prison each for targeting U.S. companies in BlackCat (ALPHV) ransomware attacks. [...]

articles.title: 1,800 Hit in Mini Shai-Hulud Attack on SAP, Lightning, Intercom
articles.summary: The compromised Lightning and Intercom packages have a combined monthly download count of nearly 10 million. The post 1,800 Hit in Mini Shai-Hulud Attack on SAP, Lightning, Intercom appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Hackers earning millions from hijacked cargo, FBI says
articles.summary: In an advisory this week, FBI officials said cyber actors have spent the last two years breaking into the systems of brokers and carriers — allowing them to pose as victim companies and post fraudulent listings on freight delivery message boards.

articles.title: New Bluekit phishing service includes an AI assistant, 40 templates
articles.summary: A new phishing kit named Bluekit offers more than 40 templates targeting popular services and includes basic AI features for generating campaign drafts. [...]

articles.title: Anthropic Unveils Claude Security to Counter AI-Powered Exploit Surge
articles.summary: With Mythos signaling a new era of near-instant exploitation, Anthropic positions Claude Security to help defenders keep pace. The post Anthropic Unveils Claude Security to Counter AI-Powered Exploit Surge appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: AI Fuels ‘Industrial’ Cybercrime as Time-to-Exploit Shrinks to Hours
articles.summary: Industrialized cybercrime delivers attacks with greater scale, speed and success. Defenders must match this with use of AI and automation. The post AI Fuels ‘Industrial’ Cybercrime as Time-to-Exploit Shrinks to Hours appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: FBI links cybercriminals to sharp surge in cargo theft attacks
articles.summary: The U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) warned the transportation and logistics industry of a sharp rise in cyber-enabled cargo theft, with estimated losses in the United States and Canada reaching nearly $725 million in 2025. [...]

articles.title: PyTorch Lightning and Intercom-client Hit in Supply Chain Attacks to Steal Credentials
articles.summary: In yet another software supply chain attack, threat actors have managed to compromise the popular Python package Lightning to push two malicious versions to conduct credential theft. According to Aikido Security, OX Security, Socket, and StepSecurity, the two malicious versions are versions 2.6.2 an

articles.title: France investigates 15-year-old over alleged hack of national ID agency
articles.summary: The minor was taken into police custody on April 25 on suspicion of involvement in a data breach affecting the National Agency for Secure Documents (ANTS), which processes applications for passports, national identity cards, residence permits and driver’s licenses.

articles.title: SonicWall Urges Immediate Patching of Firewall Vulnerabilities
articles.summary: The bugs could be exploited to bypass security controls, access restricted services, and crash firewalls. The post SonicWall Urges Immediate Patching of Firewall Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: SAP NPM Packages Targeted in Supply Chain Attack
articles.summary: The Mini Shai-Hulud attack introduced a preinstall hook to fetch and execute a Bun binary and bypass security monitoring. The post SAP NPM Packages Targeted in Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
articles.summary: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned.
